news.t: DCRE to Change Name to DoubleLine Commercial Real Estate Debt ETF
DoubleLine Launches New Exchange-Traded Funds DMBS and DCMB

overview.description: The DoubleLine Commercial Real Estate Debt ETF (DCRE) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in investment grade fixed income. The fund is an actively managed portfolio of investment grade commercial real estate debt securities of any maturity. The fund seeks current income and capital appreciation. DCRE was launched on Mar 31, 2023 and is issued by DoubleLine.

data.title: Why commercial real estate may be the winner of the pain in private equity
data.text: CNBC's Diana Olick reports on news regarding an unexpected winner of the woes in private credit.

data.title: DCRE to Change Name to DoubleLine Commercial Real Estate Debt ETF
data.text: TAMPA, Fla., Jan. 22, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Effective Feb. 2, 2026, the name of exchange-traded fund listed under the NYSE Arca ticker symbol DCRE will change to DoubleLine Commercial Real Estate Debt ...

data.title: The second-half playbook for ETFs: Will investors return to stock-based ETFs?
data.text: Todd Sohn, Strategas Securities, joins CNBC's Bob Pisani to discuss the lack of investor enthusiasm for stock-based ETFs, and how investors can prepare for the second half of 2023.
